finding: Served-hostname evidence must not be accepted independently of Cloudflare control-plane hostname authority and exact deployment metadata. A lookalike HTTPS host can serve matching identity content while remaining unbound to the intended Pages project.
design_implication: Require one fail-closed cross-artifact verdict binding the authority record, hostname evidence gate, exact deployment metadata, project name, deployment URL, hostname, and full source commit before deployment evidence is eligible for acceptance.

cases: exact authority, gate, metadata, project, URL, hostname, and source commit agreement accepted
custom-domain relation rejected until separately admitted by policy
verification.limit: The isolated runtime used byte-equivalent validator logic. The committed repository test file has not yet run in GitHub Actions or a clean checkout.

falsification_route: Run the committed tests in a clean checkout, then construct a deployment evidence set where the served page matches but the authority or metadata URL, hostname, project, or commit differs. Any accepted verdict falsifies the gate.
remaining_limit: No Cloudflare deployment occurred because required environment credentials remain externally unconfigured. This artifact does not establish a resolving hostname or successful deployment.
